summary: The kernel spec states that consequential boundaries require explicit claim, denial, and citation-bearing artifacts.

artifact_span.text: No consequential action or refusal boundary is complete without a Claim bundle and a ProofBudgetSnapshot.

observed_units.normalized_text: No consequential action or refusal boundary is complete without a Claim bundle and a ProofBudgetSnapshot.

artifact_span.text: No negative consequential boundary is complete without a Denial.

observed_units.normalized_text: No negative consequential boundary is complete without a Denial.

artifact_span.text: State-of-world or shipped-status claims in repo docs must cite a code path, schema, or test.

observed_units.normalized_text: State-of-world or shipped-status claims in repo docs must cite a code path, schema, or test.

compression.compressed_summary: Kernel constitutional rules preserve three clean promotable claims covering consequential boundary completeness and citation requirements.

truth_verification.notes: Calibration validates observation behavior and internal support only, not external truth.
